title: tephroseris hyperborealis subsp. wrangelica
intro: Tephroseris hyperborealis subsp. wrangelica, also known as Wrangel Island Tephroseris, is a rare, alpine flowering plant native to the Russian Far East. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been found to aid in the treatment of various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and skin conditions. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its unique composition of bioactive compounds, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant, antiviral, and antibacterial activities. Herbal preparations of Wrangel Island Tephroseris, often in the form of teas, infusions, or tinctures, are used to harness its medicinal benefits and promote overall well-being.
